# Test Review: daily_os_next/agents/service/

**Scope:** `lib/features/daily_os_next/agents/service/` (3 impl files) and
`test/features/daily_os_next/agents/service/` (3 test files)

*Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)*

---

## File size / shape

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lib/.../service/day_agent_plan_service.dart` | 1879 | Yes | Massively oversized; 7+ seams for extraction |
| `lib/.../service/day_agent_capture_service.dart` | 1043 | Yes | Oversized; pure helpers and I/O woven together |
| `lib/.../service/day_agent_service.dart` | 399 | Yes | Acceptable |
| `test/.../service/day_agent_plan_service_test.dart` | 4231 | — | Largest test in scope; 7 distinct `seedPlan` clones |
| `test/.../service/day_agent_capture_service_test.dart` | 2241 | — | Oversized; setUp block is 90+ lines |
| `test/.../service/day_agent_service_test.dart` | 957 | — | Acceptable |

- [ ] [HIGH] Split `day_agent_plan_service.dart` (1879 lines)

Concrete extraction seams (all clean compile-time boundaries):

1. **Parsing layer** – static helpers `_parsePlannedBlock`, `_parseEnergyBand`,
   `_parseDiffChange`, `_optionalBlockSnapshot`, `_assertWithinDay`,
   `_parseEnergyBand`, `_objectList`, `_stringList`, `_requiredString`,
   `_requiredDateTime`, `_optionalString`, `_optionalInt`, `_optionalDateTime`,
   `_optionalEnum`, `_blankToNull`, `_optionalIntList`
   → extract to `day_agent_plan_parser.dart` (~350 lines).

2. **Diff resolution pipeline** – `_resolvePlanDiff`, `_validateApplicableBatch`,
   `_applyItem`, `_stateForAcceptedAddedBlock`, `_scheduledMinutesFor`,
   `_pinnedTasksFor`, `_selectIndices`, `_formatChangeSummary`, plus the private
   classes `_DiffChange`, `_BlockSnapshot`, `_DiffAction`
   → extract to `day_agent_plan_diff.dart` (~430 lines).

3. **Read projections** – `draftPlanForDay`, `pendingPlanDiffsForDay`,
   `deletePlanForDay`, `_dayIdFromPlanEntityId`
   → stays slim core (~180 lines after extractions).

4. **Tool dispatch façade** – `executeTool` + private `_draftDayPlanTool`,
   `_summarizeRecentPatternsTool`, `_proposePlanDiffTool`, `_acceptPlanDiffTool`,
   `_revertPlanDiffTool`, `_commitDayTool`, `_uncommitDayTool`, `_resolutionSummary`,
   `_planJson`, `_blockJson`
   → extract to `day_agent_plan_tool_dispatcher.dart` (~200 lines).

Mirror-split the test file into at least 3 files matching the above seams. The
current 4231-line test file is the largest in the codebase and already exceeds
CI shard time budgets in proportion to the number of tests it contains.

- [ ] [HIGH] Split `day_agent_capture_service.dart` (1043 lines)

Extract the FTS matching + corpus-snapshot logic (`matchToCorpus`,
`buildTaskCorpusSnapshot`, `_categoryFilterForHint`, `_maxCorpusTasks`,
`_maxMatchCandidates`) into `day_agent_corpus_service.dart` (~200 lines).
Extract the task-triage pipeline (`applyTriage`, `_endOfDay`, task status
transitions) into `day_agent_triage_service.dart` (~180 lines). The remaining
capture + reconcile core sits at ~650 lines — still large but single-purpose.

- [ ] [MED] Split `day_agent_plan_service_test.dart` (4231 lines)

Seven independent `seedPlan` helper definitions (lines 951, 1472, 2730, 2999,
3208, 3923, 4004) each produce structurally identical `DayPlanEntity` values.
When the impl file is split, the test file must be split with it. Even without
that, the test file's group-level `seedPlan` / `seedChangeSet` helpers could be
hoisted to a module-level factory so each sub-group file does not re-implement
them.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] [HIGH] Extract a shared `seedPlan` + `seedChangeSet` helper — plan service test — **RESOLVED:** one module-level `seedPlanEntity({status, blocks, capacityMinutes, scheduledMinutes, deletedAt})` + `seedChangeSetEntity({items, id})` mutate the shared `agentEntities` map; all seven group-local `seedPlan`/`seedPlanWithStatus`/`seedChangeSet` definitions are now thin wrappers preserving their exact per-group defaults (4231 → ~3990 lines).

There are **7 distinct `seedPlan` definitions** (lines 951, 1472, 2730, 2999,
3208, 3923, 4004) and a matching `seedChangeSet` (line 1502) repeated across
`proposePlanDiff`, `acceptPlanDiff / revertPlanDiff`, `commitDay`,
`uncommitDay`, `executeTool dispatch for refine`, `acceptPlanDiff on
agreed-status plan`, and `deletePlanForDay` groups. Every one creates nearly
identical `AgentDomainEntity.dayPlan(...)` boilerplate. Hoist a single
module-level `_seedPlan({DayPlanStatus, List<PlannedBlock>, String agentId})`
and `_seedChangeSet({List<ChangeItem>})` that mutates the shared `agentEntities`
map, eliminating ~600 lines of copy-paste setup.

- [x] [HIGH] Large `ChangeItem` inline constructions in plan service test (lines ~1948–1978, ~1994–2015, ~2047–2060, ~2293–2320, etc.) — **RESOLVED (partially assessed):** `moveBlockItem`/`addBlockItem`/`dropBlockItem` are lifted to module scope (moveBlockItem gained a `type` override) and the agreed-status group's inline change-set now uses them via `seedChangeSetEntity(id:, items:)`. Most remaining inline `ChangeItem`s are deliberately malformed/bespoke fixtures (missing categoryId, forbidden category, pre-resolved copies) whose explicitness is the point of those tests.

About **28 inline `ChangeItem(...)` constructions** (verified by grep) appear
directly in test bodies alongside `seedChangeSet`. These should become named
helpers (extending the existing `moveBlockItem()`, `addBlockItem()`,
`dropBlockItem()` defined in the `acceptPlanDiff / revertPlanDiff` group) and
be lifted to module scope so all groups can share them.

- [ ] [MED] Capture service test: 90-line `setUp` block (lines 148–242)

The `setUp` block registers 10+ `when(...)` stubs for all possible DB/repo
method permutations. Most tests only exercise 2–3 paths; the blanket stubs for
`getOpenTasksForDayAgentCorpus`, `getTasksDueOn`, `getTasksDueOnOrBefore`,
`getInProgressTasks`, `getMissedRecurringTasks`, `getJournalEntitiesForIdsUnordered`,
`watchFullTextMatches`, `getCategoryById`, `journalEntityById` are reasonable as
a base. However, the custom `taskFactory` closure (lines 103–145) duplicates
`_task` construction logic already in the test file. Extract a named
`_makeTaskFactory(journalEntities)` helper so this is defined once.

- [ ] [MED] Plan service test: two assertions per test at lines ~732–774

The test `'executeTool rejects blocks missing required fields'` (line 732)
runs two sequential `createService().executeTool(...)` calls inside one test
body to check `missingStart` and `missingTitle`. Per AGENTS.md this is a
copy-paste permutation — split into two named tests or parameterize:

```dart
for (final (label, args, fragment) in [
  ('missing start', {...}, 'start must be a valid ISO-8601'),
  ('missing title', {...}, 'title must not be empty'),
]) {
  test('executeTool rejects block with $label', () async { ... });
}
```

- [ ] [MED] Plan service test: `'persistDraftPlan rejects captures owned by a different agent'` test (line 589)

Asserts only `throwsA(isA<DayAgentCaptureException>())` without checking the
message. Every similar test in the same file uses `.having((e) => e.message, 'message', contains(...))`.
Weak assertion; add `.having(...)`.

- [ ] [LOW] Capture service test: `'executeTool dispatches link, break, surface, and apply tools'` (line 958)

A single test body chains 4 sequential tool dispatches with independent
assertions. The 75-line test body should be split into 4 focused tests. Mock
state mutation between dispatch calls makes the combined test fragile to
ordering.

- [ ] [LOW] Plan service test: `'persistDraftPlan sorts equal-start blocks by id…'` (line 631)

Two assertions are bundled: sort order AND `createdAt` reuse. These are
independent behaviors; two separate tests would make regression attribution
clearer.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

No existing Glados tests in this subdir (confirmed by grep).

- [ ] [MED] `_categoryAllowed(categoryId, allowed)` — `day_agent_plan_service.dart` line 1346

Pure function with two independent boolean degrees of freedom: `allowed` is
null/empty (permit-all) vs. non-empty set, `categoryId` is null vs.
present-and-matching vs. present-and-foreign. A Glados property could assert:
"when `allowed` is empty, always returns `true`; when `allowed` is non-empty,
returns `true` iff `categoryId` is in `allowed`." Candidate generator shape:
`(Set<String> allowed, String? categoryId)`.

```dart
Glados2(any.setOfString, any.nullableString)
    .test('_categoryAllowed satisfies permit-all invariant', (allowed, id) {
  // ...
}, tags: 'glados');
```

- [ ] [MED] `_selectIndices(itemIndices, itemCount)` — line 1387

Pure function with invariants: when `itemIndices` is null returns `[0..n-1]`;
when explicit, rejects negatives and out-of-range; deduplicates and sorts.
Glados could drive `(itemIndices: List<int>?, itemCount: int)` and assert the
length/sort/dedup invariants hold on any valid input.

- [ ] [MED] `_blankToNull(value)` — line 1340

`null` input → `null`; blank-only string → `null`; non-blank → trimmed value.
Round-trip: for any non-blank string `s`, `_blankToNull(s.trim()) == s.trim()`.
Trivial Glados property on `any.string`.

- [ ] [LOW] `_scheduledMinutesFor(blocks)` — line 1241

Additive fold: sum of `duration.inMinutes` for non-dropped blocks.
Glados property: `_scheduledMinutesFor([] + [droppedBlock]) == _scheduledMinutesFor([])`.
Non-trivial only if there are future changes to the dropping logic.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] [HIGH] `_applyItem` with `'move_block'` clearing taskId (no `taskId` key in args) — **RESOLVED:** new test seeds a block with `taskId: 'task-keep'`, applies a `move_block` whose args carry no `taskId` key, and asserts both the move and the retained taskId.

`_applyItem` has three branches for `taskId` inside `move_block`: key absent
(retain), key present with `null` value (clear), key present with string value
(replace). The test at line 2543 covers the `null`-clear case, but there is
**no test for the key-absent retain path** independently of clock or other
mutations. The partial-move test at line 2382 (only `toEnd`) implicitly retains
`taskId`, but does not assert on `taskId` value. Add a targeted assertion.

- [x] [HIGH] `acceptPlanDiff` with `move_block` that changes `type` via `args['type']` — **RESOLVED:** new test applies `moveBlockItem(type: 'manual')` and asserts the moved block's type flips to `PlannedBlockType.manual`.

`_applyItem` calls `_argType(args) ?? block.type` to optionally override block
type. No test exercises a `move_block` that carries a `type` override. Add a
test asserting the block's type is updated.

- [ ] [MED] `summarizeRecentPatterns` with `lookbackDays` other than 7

Only `lookbackDays: 7` (default) and `lookbackDays: 0` (error path) are tested.
Missing: `lookbackDays: 1` (edge: only today; `yesterdayPlan` will be null),
and a case where the plan set spans the exact boundary day.

- [ ] [MED] `deletePlanForDay` with multiple inbound links mixing live and soft-deleted

Only tested with 0 links (line 4036), 1 live link (line 4079), and 1 deleted
link (line 4127). Missing: plan with **2 live + 1 deleted** links — verifies the
loop condition `if (link.deletedAt != null) continue` under iteration.

- [ ] [MED] `persistDraftPlan` with `capacityMinutes <= 0`

The validation `if (capacityMinutes <= 0)` at line 694 of the impl is not
tested. Add `persistDraftPlan(... capacityMinutes: 0)` expecting
`DayAgentCaptureException`.

- [ ] [MED] `proposePlanDiff` rejects a non-map change element

`_parseDiffChange` throws when `raw is! Map` (line 1412 of impl). This error
path has no dedicated test — only malformed-but-map inputs are tested.

- [ ] [LOW] `_stateForAcceptedAddedBlock` `orElse` branch

The default `orElse: () => PlannedBlockState.drafted` handles statuses that are
neither `agreed` nor `committed`. Tests cover `draft`, `committed`, and `agreed`
only. A plan in `DayPlanStatus.needsReview` (or another legacy variant) would
exercise the `orElse` branch — add one test.

- [ ] [LOW] `buildTaskCorpusSnapshot` with inProgress tasks

`DayAgentCaptureService.buildTaskCorpusSnapshot` calls
`getInProgressTasks(categoryIds:)` but none of the existing tests seed in-progress
tasks into its result (confirmed: the stub always returns `[]`). Add one test.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

No real `Future.delayed`, `sleep`, or `Timer` calls were found in this test
subdir (confirmed by grep). All time-sensitive tests use `Clock.fixed` +
`withClock`. No `pumpAndSettle` (no widget tests). Setup overhead per test is
modest given the in-memory map-based repository stubs.

- [ ] [MED] Plan service test: per-test `createService()` instantiation (all 88 top-level calls)

Every test calls `createService()` which re-instantiates `DayAgentPlanService`
and its `Uuid()` instance. The service is stateless between tests (state lives
in `agentEntities` map). Consider hoisting `service` to a `late` variable
initialized in `setUp`, matching the capture service test pattern. Impact: minor
— dart object construction is fast, but reducing 88 per-test factory calls
removes noise.

- [ ] [LOW] `setUp` in capture service test re-stubs 10 methods unconditionally

All 65 tests incur the full 10-stub registration every time, even when only
2–3 are exercised. If the test file is split per extraction seam, each split
file can register only its relevant stubs in `setUp`. Estimated 10–15% speedup
per shard after split.

---

## Summary

- **2 oversized impl files**: `day_agent_plan_service.dart` (1879 lines) and `day_agent_capture_service.dart` (1043 lines) both exceed the 1000-line hard limit; the plan service exceeds it by nearly 2×.
- **2 oversized test files**: `day_agent_plan_service_test.dart` (4231 lines, 88 tests) and `day_agent_capture_service_test.dart` (2241 lines, 65 tests).
- **3 critical test-quality gaps**: 7 copy-pasted `seedPlan` definitions; ~28 inline `ChangeItem` constructions not shared across groups; `setUp` in capture test exceeds 90 lines and should have a named factory helper extracted.
- **4 Glados candidates**: `_categoryAllowed`, `_selectIndices`, `_blankToNull`, `_scheduledMinutesFor` are all pure static functions with clear algebraic invariants and no I/O.
- **7 coverage gaps**: notably missing: `move_block` type-override path, key-absent `taskId` retain path, `capacityMinutes <= 0` guard, non-map change element, multi-link `deletePlanForDay`, `summarizeRecentPatterns` edge days, `orElse` block-state branch.
- **Biggest single opportunity**: split `day_agent_plan_service.dart` into parsing / diff-resolution / read-projections / tool-dispatch with a matching test split — would bring the largest file from 1879 lines to four files under 450 lines each and the test file from 4231 to four files under 1100 lines each, staying within the AGENTS.md `< ~500` / `< 1000` guidance.
